# b00tl3gRSA3 [Hard] (by invisibility) - picoCTF 2019
> Why use p and q when I can use more? Connect with <code>nc jupiter.challenges.picoctf.org 3726</code>.

Use sage to find out all the factor and find out the phi(n) based on the factor:
```python
c = 
n = 
e = 65537

factor_list = [8763065357,9817126757,9837727789,10137381743,10144220753,10468982071,10557783983,
               10595883997,10681394513,10789575649,10830755863,11137557281,11214710329,11602706693,
               11716826081,12601777897,12616828561,12739780513,13234403263,14092039217,14248620949,
               14706823111,14782879051,14856626263,15047967569,15168775093,15197087131,15217143761,
               15397846913,15561277157,15817184323,16416239501,16480994339,16580644849]

phi = 1
for i in factor_list:
    phi = phi * (i-1)

d = inverse(e, phi)
print(long_to_bytes(pow(c,d,n)).decode())
```
With phi, get the private_key d and recover the message.

flag: `picoCTF{too_many_fact0rs_8606199}`
